english: time period; time; moment; length of time

etymology.description: 時(時) (shí, "time; hour; period") refers to a specific point in time or a duration. 候(候) (hòu, "wait; moment") indicates a time or a period during which something can occur.
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 時(時) (shí, "time; hour; period") and 候(候) (hòu, "wait; moment") suggests a span of time or a specific moment when something happens. It reflects how a moment is both a point and a period in time.

examples.chinese: 我走的时候(我走的時候)，天已经黑了(天已經黑了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zǒu de shíhòu, tiān yǐjīng hēi le.
examples.english: By the time I left, it was already dark.

examples.chinese: 上学的时候(上學的時候)，他喜欢写作(他喜歡寫作)。
examples.pinyin: Shàngxué de shíhòu, tā xǐhuān xiězuò.
examples.english: When he was in school, he liked writing.

examples.chinese: 你什么时候回来(你什麼時候回來)？
